---
title: "Roadmap"
---

How every module depends on the others. Blue nodes are reusable
[foundations](../../foundations/README.md) modules shared across courses; the green
node is a whole earlier course; arrows mean "needed for".

Like every course, this one assumes the notation grammar in
[reading-math-notation](../../foundations/reading-math-notation/lesson.qmd) — take it
first if the symbols aren't yet second nature.

This course also **builds on** [Simple Linear Regression from Scratch](../simple-linear-regression/syllabus.md)
(fitting one predictor by least squares; reading `lm()` output) and reuses its anchors
rather than re-teaching them; if rusty, skim its syllabus first.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    classDef foundation fill:#e8f0fe,stroke:#4285f4
    classDef priorcourse fill:#e6f4ea,stroke:#34a853

    F1["linear-combinations-and-data-matrices<br/>(design matrix X, linear combinations)"]:::foundation
    F2["matrices-and-linear-transforms<br/>(Xᵀ, XᵀX, solve, inverse)"]:::foundation
    F3["variance-structure-and-standardization<br/>(correlation between predictors)"]:::foundation
    F4["partial-derivatives-and-the-gradient<br/>(set every partial slope to 0)"]:::foundation

    C1["course: simple-linear-regression<br/>(the line β₀+β₁x; reading lm())"]:::priorcourse

    M1["01 · Many knobs, one model<br/>(y = β₀+β₁x₁+…+βₚxₚ, 'others held fixed')"]
    M2["02 · Least squares & the normal equations<br/>(XᵀX β̂ = Xᵀy)"]
    M3["03 · Marginal vs joint<br/>(the vanishing / sign-flipping coefficient)"]
    M4["04 · Capstone<br/>(decode a full summary(lm()))"]

    F2 --> F1
    F2 --> F3

    C1 --> M1
    F1 --> M1
    M1 --> M2
    F2 --> M2
    F4 --> M2
    M2 --> M3
    F3 --> M3
    M3 --> M4
```

## Suggested order

1. Foundation: [linear-combinations-and-data-matrices](../../foundations/linear-combinations-and-data-matrices/lesson.qmd) —
   new? take it fully; done before? skim its pronunciation table.
2. Foundation: [matrices-and-linear-transforms](../../foundations/matrices-and-linear-transforms/lesson.qmd) —
   same rule.
3. Foundation: [variance-structure-and-standardization](../../foundations/variance-structure-and-standardization/lesson.qmd) —
   same rule.
4. Foundation: [partial-derivatives-and-the-gradient](../../foundations/partial-derivatives-and-the-gradient/lesson.qmd) —
   same rule (you likely met this in the earlier regression course).
5. [01 · Many knobs, one model](modules/01-many-knobs-one-model/lesson.qmd)
   (+ [practice](modules/01-many-knobs-one-model/practice.qmd))
6. [02 · Least squares and the normal equations](modules/02-least-squares-and-the-normal-equations/lesson.qmd)
   (+ [practice](modules/02-least-squares-and-the-normal-equations/practice.qmd))
7. [03 · Marginal vs joint](modules/03-marginal-vs-joint/lesson.qmd)
   (+ [practice](modules/03-marginal-vs-joint/practice.qmd))
8. [04 · Capstone — reading a multiple regression](modules/04-capstone-reading-a-multiple-regression/lesson.qmd)
   (+ [practice](modules/04-capstone-reading-a-multiple-regression/practice.qmd))

After each lesson, do its practice page before moving on — the problems are the part
that makes it stick. External videos/readings for each module: [resources](resources.md).
